and daksha-kanyā, s.f. 'Daughter of Daksha,' Durgā (called also Pārvatī, Devī, Satī, &c.).

r: dāl, da, dāl-ě-muhmala, ta-varg, d, abjad, da-kār
def: s.m. The letter or sound da.
more: A. is the 8th letter of the Arabic, the 10th of the Persian, and the 11th of the Hindustānī alphabet. It is the 8th consonant in the Hindī alphabet and the third letter of the तवर्ग or dental class. In the numerical application of the Arabic alphabet, it stands for four; in almanacs, it represents the fourth day of the week, Wednesday. It is also used in Astronomy for the planet Mercury, or the sign Leo of the Zodiac. As an initial, medial, and final it is interchangeable with b, t, j, ch , q. v.: with k , as Panj. dā = H. kā of; usdā = uskā his; dhāṅsnā = khāṅsnā to cough; chhed = chhek a hole. As an initial and medial, with l , as dhat = lat bad habit; P. dag = P. lag barren land; kurednā = kurelnā to overturn; gudgudī = gulgulī tickling; H. bolnā = Gaṛh. bodnā to speak. As an initial and final, with g , as dhasnā = ghusnā to penetrate; pad = pag foot: with s , as bakvād = bakvās jabbe…

It is the 13th consonant of the Hindī alphabet, and the third letter of the टवर्ग or cerebral class. It has no corresponding sound or character in Arabic or Persian. It is pronounced by applying the tip of the tongue inverted to the palate. Its sound is very nearly that of our own d in did . It is sometimes used for d (د) and vice versa, as डण्ड for दण्ड. It is interchangeable as an initial, medial, and final with ṭ q. v. : with g , as ḍālnā=ghālnā to put in; ḍālnā=gernā to throw; umaṅḍnā=umaṅgnā to overflow; lanḍ=liṅg, membrum virile : with d , as ḍaṅk =S daṅs sting; ḍaṅd =S dand fine; Eng day =H. din : W. ḍāṛhī = E. dāṛhī beard; H. ḍar =S. dar fear; Lat. ḍo = H. do give; Eng. baḍ=P. bad ; Eng. ḍaughter=P. dukhtar . As an initial and medial with l , as ḍālnā = lāṛnā to throw; ḍūr=lār a row; S. taḍāk = H talāo , a tank. As a medial and final with ṛ , as gaḍh=gaṛh a fort; ḍāṅḍ=ḍ/?/ṅṛ oar;…

is the eighth letter of the Arabic, and the tenth of the Persian alphabet. In the representation of Hindī words by the letters of these alphabets, it denotes the Nāgarī द ( da ); or, with ه ( he ) subjoined, ध ( dha ). In the numerical application of the Arabic alphabet, د ( dāl ) stands for four; hence, in almanacks, it represents the fourth day of the week, Wednesday: it is, moreover, used in astronomy to denote the planet Mercury, or the sign Leo of the Zodiack. This letter, in Persian, may be changed to ت ( te ); as, in بتر for بدتر Worse: or, when the next preceding letter is ,و,ا, or ي, or is moveable by a short vowel, it may be converted to ذ ( ẕāl ); as استاذ for استاد A master, گنبذ for گنبد A dome: (see Lumsdenʼs Persian Gram. p. 24). And, in Arabic words of the measure افتعال, when ,ذ,د or ز is the first letter of the root, د ( dāl ) is substituted for the servile ت ( te ); as…

more: n. m. S. दारिद्र, Pālī, daliddo . 1. Poverty; pauperism (افلاس). 1. Uddam kare, daliddar bhāge . Prov. Your hands but ply and poverty will fly. 2. Īshvar paiṭho! dāriddar niklo! Come wealth! go poverty! 3. Jib ham khetī kāḷan lāge, Pāp, daliddar sagre bhāge . Wom. Rus. song. Strike a sickle in the wheat, sin and sorrow both retreat. 4. Meṅh na barastā to abhī ke diladdar pār thā . Grain-sellers. If it had n't rained I should have been rich. 5. Jable daliddar dādā chhipā lāvath, table hamrā bhuīëṅ meṅ dā . Mag. Prov. While Father Poverty is bringing a plate, give me some food on the ground. 2. Rubbish; sweepings; refuse. Divālī kā daliddar . daliddar pār honā , v. n. To be banished (poverty). diladdar nikāl'nā, yā khednā , v. a. To take a sieve or winnowing basket and beat it in every corner of the house for the purpose of driving out poverty. This custom is observed on the morning of Div…

def: v.t. To throw, fling, cast, hurl, drop; to produce or have an abortion of; to shed, pour, inject; to vomit; to place or lay (before), to submit, to present; to lay (on), to put or throw (on, as a garment, &c.); to keep (as a mistress), to put aside; to push; to set; to shake; to scatter, to sow broadcast; to sow (discord, &c.); to thrust; to destroy; to protrude; to involve; to cause, occasion, produce, excite: (ḍālnā annexed to the base of another verb, is used to form intensives, e.g. toṛ-ḍālnā, 'To break to pieces'; de-ḍālnā, 'To give away'; khā-ḍālnā, 'To eat up the whole')
more: v. a. To throw down, to fling, cast, drop, shed, pour, inject, lay, put, push, set, shake, submit, throw, hurl, thrust, destroy, throw away; cause, occasion, produce, excite. ڐال دينا ḍāl-denā, v. a. To throw away, cast, cause, occasion, &c.

v.t. To put away, lay aside; to detain

def: v. a. 1. In comp . as a helping verb, implying completion of the act, as khā ḍālnā , to eat up every bit. 2. To place; throw; drop; let fall. 1. Ḍāl dīje, dekhā kījī! Riddle. Ans. A Chiq screen. 2. Patthar ḍāre kīck meṅ, uchhṛe, bigāṛe aṅg . Prov. The stone you throw into the mire will splash and spoil your own attire. 3. To miscarry; abort. Gābh ḍāl dīyā . 4. To vomit (اُبکنا). 5. To fling ( pheṅknā , 1). 6. To put on; wear. Aṅgarkhā gale meṅ ḍāl lo . 7. To thrust in; drive in. 8. To lay before one; tender; present. 9. To entertain; keep as a mistress. 10. To lay on; impose; levy. ḍāl jānā , v. n. 1. To leave behind; throw away. 2. To leave; abandon; desert. ḍāl rakhnā , v. n. To cover (an animal). sūt, dhāgā, yā ḍorā ḍāl'nā , v. a. To thread. khalal yā futūr ḍālnā , To affect injuriously; to cause mischief or disturbance.

def: s.m. Money; price, value, cost (syn. mol; qīmat); — a copper coin, or a measure of money-value, equal to one twenty-fifth of a paisā (at the present time: in the time of Akbar the dām is said to have been of the value of one fortieth of a rupee)
more: n. m. S. द्रम्म 1. ( dām damṛe ) money. 1. Dām dīje, kām līje . Prov. Your money hand, work command. 2. Lage dām, bane kām . Prov. Good money good work. 3. Tumhārī girah meṅ bhī kuchh dām damṛe haiṅ? Have you any money about you ? 4. Koī kām kare dām se, ham dām kareṅ kām se . Prov. Some work with money get, I money with my labour get. 5. Nārī bulāī kharch-ke dām, tan gorā arū abran siyām, Āvat hī pardes sidhārī, pahūṅchī jahāṅ bhaī atī piyārī, Bharī gai, rīti ho āī, tab woh nārī purakh kahāī . Riddle. Ans. Hunḍī a cheque. With money I a fair one got, her body fair with black relieved, No sooner come abroad she's sent, arrived she is with joy received, Full found she went and empty came, then all in her a male perceived. 7. Dām kare sab kām . Prov. With money you can do anything. (Money makes the mare to go). 2. Price; value; rate ( qīmat ). Is kitāb ke kyā dām haiṅ? 3. A measure of mone…

s. m. 1. The twenty-fourth part of a paisā. 2. Price.

ety: v.n. fr. dam-īdan, rt. Zend dam = S. धम्˚
def: s.m. Breath, vital air, life; — a moment, an instant; — breath or blast (of a furnace or oven); a puff, whiff, pull, draw (of a ḥuqqa); a draught (of water); stewing or simmering over a slow fire; — spring, elasticity; vitality, energy, vigour, stamina, spirit, mettle; strength, goodness, virtue (as of medicines, cloth, &c.); — ambition; — cheerfulness, pleasure, recreation; society (as a breathing together); — edge (of a sword); point (of a spear)
more: n. m. 1. Breath; life. See جلن (1.). 1. Dam Qalandar, dūdh malīdā! A devotee before you capering see, with milk and cakes just feed him free! 2. Ek da/?/n meṅ hazār dam . Prov. On one life a thousand lives depend. 3. Dam kā kyā bharosā? Prov. Life is uncertain. 4. Jab tak dam hai, tab tak gam hai . Prov. While there is life there is sorrow. 5. Woh apne dam se achchhā hai . Prov. saying. He is a good man. 6. Khālā kā dam, aur kivāṛ kī joṛī . Slang. Prov. Only my aunt and a pair of doors! (The cool reply of a man who was told that his house was being plundered by thieves). 7. Amīr ko jān piyārī, faqīr ko ek dam bhārī . Prov. def: v.t. To cause to move or vibrate, to shake.
more: v. a. To shake (as, the branch of a tree).

def: s.f. An assessment
more: s. m. An assessment. دامي لگانا dāmī lagānā, To assess.
s. m. A setter of snares, a hunter or fowler.
subs: s.f. Gross assets of a village.

ety: dām, q.v.+ī = Zend aêna = S. इन्
def: s.m. A setter of snares, a fowler, a hunter, sportsman.
s.f. A kind of ḥuqqa, or pipe (for smoking).
more: n. f. P. dam breath. A small huqqā , or pipe.
s. f. A kind of ḥuḳḳa, instrument or pipe for smoking.

ety: perf. part. of damīdan, rt. Zend dam = S. धम˚, rt. ध्मा
def: part. Blown, blossomed, shot forth, opened out, expanded, vegetated (a plant); blowing, blossoming; sprouting; broken forth (as the dawn of day).
more: (from دميدن) part. Blown (a flower), shot forth, vegetated (a plant).

def: s.f. Blowing (as of wind); blowing (of a flower), blossoming; sprouting.
more: (from دميده) s. f. 1. Blowing (as, of wind). 2. Blowing (of a flower), sprouting.

def: s.f. A heroine, &c. (see damak).
s.f. Lightning.
adj. (dakh.) Rich, wealthy.
s.m. (local) A hillock, an eminence.
more: n. m. A hillock; an eminence (?).

def: s.m. A squirt; a pump; a fire-engine; — a jack or crane (for raising weights).
s.m. A squirt; a pump; a fire-engine; — a jack or crane (for raising weights).
more: n. m. S. दम to press कला a machine. 1. A pump. 2. A fire engine. 3. A jack or crane. (?).
s. m. 1. A squirt, a fire-engine. 2. A jack, or crane (for raising weights).

def: s. (dakh.) Asthma ( Harris ).

s.m. (lit. 'Let it not breathe'), A religious ceremony very popular with the agricultural and lower classes in Upper Hindūstān, which consists in jumping into a fire and treading it out with the exclamation of dam-madār! dam-madār! (cf. dhammāl).
v.n. To glow, to sparkle, glitter, flash; — to be tempered (as steel); to bend with elasticity, to spring (in these senses, prob. formed from dam, q.v.).
more: v. n. 1. ( damaknā ) To glitter; flash. 2. (P. dam ) To be tempered.
v. n. 1. To glitter, to flash. 2. To bend with elasticity, to spring.

r: dāmodar, dāma, udara, Kr̤ishṇa, lit, Yaśodā
def: (dāma+udara), prop. n. Name of Kr̤ishṇa; a proper name; (lit. 'Having a rope round the belly'; Yaśodā, the foster-mother of Kr̤ishṇa, having in vain passed a rope round his body, whilst a child, to keep him in confinement).
s.m. (local) The tree Xanthochynus pictorius or Stalagmites pictoria (called also de-phūl).
more: n. m. Stalagmites pictoria; xanthochynus pictorius . Wat.

def: s.m. Wife and husband, master and mistress, the couple.
s.m. Master or lord of the house; master, lord.
s.m. Gold, silver, money, wealth, riches
subs: v.t. To sell off, to sell cheap.

def: s.m. The whirl of a spindle (a spherical piece of leather or wood) on which the thread is wound ( = damakṛā, damkhaṛā).
n. m. S. द्रम्म Money; wealth; riches. Hamāre pās dām na damṛe. damṛe k. , v. a. To sell cheap; sell off.
s.f. = damṛī, q.v.
more: n. f. 1. A. pie; one-fourth or 1/8 of a pice. 2. A nominal coin. 1. Damṛī kī buṛhiyā, ṭakā sir muṅḍāī . Prov. The old woman a farthing worth, and a penny to shave her head! 2. Damṛī meṅ muṅh lāl hai ! Street cry. Lips reddened for a farthing (of pān ) ho! 3. ( Met .) A farthing; a sou. 4. A sub-division of land, one damṛī = 25 kachā bīghās. damṛī ke tīn tīn , Very cheap.

def: s.f. One-fourth or one-eighth of a paisā (about three dāms); a nominal coin; — (fig.) a farthing, a sou; — a subdivision of land-measure (one damṛī = 25 kaććā bīghās).
more: s. f. The eighth part of a paisā. دمڙي کي تين تين هونا damṛī ke tīn tīn honā, To be ruined, demolished.
s. A small coin current in the Carnatic, four of which go to a pice (Qanoon-e-islam).

def: n. m. S. डमरु A kind of small drum, shaped like an hour glass.
s.m. Pain in the knee-joints.
s.m. Name of a low tribe of Rājpūts in the Benares district.
adj. To be tamed, tameable, to be subjected or controlled; tamed, subjected; — s.m. A young bullock, a steer that has to be tamed.

ety: Pehl. dān; Zend dāna; S. धान
def: An affix denoting the receptacle, place, or stand (for a thing; — e.g. pīk-dān; qalām-dān; shamʻ-dān, &c., qq.v.).

ety: fr. dānistan; Pehl. dāneśtan; rt. Zend dā, 'to know'
def: part. Knowing, understanding (used as last member of comp., e.g. qadr-dān, nukta-dān, &c., qq.v.).

corr. of dāṅw; and of dāṅt, qq.v. s.m. Time, turn; vicissitude; opportunity, chance; (fig.) power, clutch, grasp ( = qābū); — ambuscade, ambush ( = ghāt); snare, trap, stratagem, artifice, trick, manœuvre; sleight or trick (in wrestling); — a stake, wager; throw or cast (of dice); stroke (at a game)
more: n. m. S. दा to give. 1. A gift; grant; alms ( dān pun ); charity. 1. Durge! karo budh kā dān! Maiṅ mūrakh hūṅ nipaṭ nidān . Bhajan. 2. Rājā kā dān, parjā kā ashnān . Prov. The Raja's alms, and the subject's ablutions (equally efficacious). 2. (Sant. gonaṅg ) A dowry ( jahez ). 3. Alms on which a Brahman pronounces a charm before it is given away. dān-patr , n. m. One to whom a grant may be made or to whom by law property may be conveyed. dān-patr, dān-paṭṭī , n. m. A deed of gift to Brahmans. dān datār, dānī , adj. ( Ironic .) A liberal man. dān denā, dān k. , v. n. To make a gift or grant; bestow alms.
to keep. ( In Comp .) A receptacle, as qalam-dān a pen-case; shama-dān a candlestick, hulās-dānī a snuff-box.
( In Comp .) دانستن to know. Knowing; understanding, as qadr-dān one capable of appreciating.

s. m. 1. Act of giving or bestowing, gift, donation, alms, charity. 2. Any thing demanded by law or custom, as toll, &c. دان پتر दानपत्र dānpattar, s. m. A deed of conveyance or gift. دان پن dān-pun ( दानपुण्य ) s. m. Charity, alms.

1. part. act. (of دانستن) Knowing, understanding (used in compos.); as, قدردان ḳadr-dān, Knowing the value or quality (of persons). نکته دان nukta-dān, Subtile, penetrating. 2. An affix denoting the receptacle, place or stand; as, قلم دان ḳalam-dān, A pen-case. شمع دان shamﻌ-dān, A candlestick.
subs: v.n. To lie in ambush, to lurk
v.t. To bring under (one's) power; to get a grip (of one's opponent in wrestling)
dāṅʼo-par lagānā or lagā-denā, v.t. To stake, wager
v.n. To be thrown (dice); to throw high, have a good throw
(apnā), To watch (one's) opportunity; to lie in wait (for)
To make the most of an opportunity, to take advantage
v.n. To have the advantage; — dāṅʼo denā (-ko), To give (one) his turn; to trick, deceive
To practise a sleight or trick (in wrestling)
(-se), To be tricked, be taken in
v.n. To get an opportunity or chance
v.n. To take (one's) turn; — to seize an opportunity

def: s.m. = dāna, q.v. s.m. Giving, presenting, offering; giving in marriage; gift, present, donation, grant; liberality, charity, alms; anything demanded by law or by custom (as toll, &c.); — a religious rite in which the Brāhmans pronounce a charm or incantation over anything in the wish of a happy futurity and give it (dān) as a present (to another person); the thing so charmed and given away; — a dowry; — the fluid that flows from the temples of an elephant in rut
subs: s.m. 'A master of liberality,' an exceedingly liberal or munificent man
s.m. A deed of conveyance, a deed of gift by which land is conveyed to Brāhmans; — a will; — one to whom a grant may be made, or to whom by law property may be conveyed
s.m. A grantee, of Brāhman caste, to whom lands have been assigned by deed
s.f. A religious gift to a mendicant, or to a Brāhman priest
s.m. Almsgiving, charitable acts, charity; the rules or practice of almsgiving
adj. Liberally disposed, liberal, generous, charitable, munificent, bountiful
s.f. Beneficence of disposition, liberality, charitableness, &c.
v.t. To give away, to bestow alms, to give in alms; — v.n. To practise liberality, &c.
s.m. 'A hero in liberality,' an exceedingly liberal man
adj. Worthy of a gift, meriting a donation.

ety: Pehl. dānek; Zend dāna; S. dhānā
def: s.m. Grain, corn; food; — a grain; seed, berry; chick-pea, 'gram'; (local) poppy-seed; — a speck; a bead; a pimple, pustule; — dice
more: n. m. Pahlvī. and Z. dānāk . 1. ( dāna dunkā ) Grain (Sant. on ); corn; seed (Sant. eta ). 1. Dāne dāne ko mohtāj hai . Prov. Saying. 2. Dāne dāne par muhar hai . Prov. On every grain a seal. (Destiny has sealed up every grain of food, to be opened only by the man for whom it is provided). 3. Dānā khā moṭh kā, pānī pī soṅṭh kā . Boys'rhyme. Eat moṭh gram and you 'll drink ginger water. 2. Food ( āb dānā 1.). 3. W. Gram. 4. (E) Poppy seed. 5. Rations. 6. Bead. Tasbīh ke dānoṅ pe nazar kar, nādāṅ, Gardish men sadā rahtā hai jo dānā hai . Observe, thou fool! how rise and fall the beads the pious tell, Even so the wise all in a whirl of anxious thought do dwell. 7. Dice (Gamblers). 8. A pimple; pustule; pock; small pox; chicken pox. dāna ugalvānā , v. a. To cause the pigeon to bring up the grain. This is done, it is said, to make the pigeon light, for flying, and sometimes for the accumulati…

s. m. A grain, berry, seed, corn, speck, pimple. دانه بدلنا dāna badalnā, v. a. To caress as birds do by joining bills, to bill. دانه بدلول dāna-badlauwal, Billing, caressing as birds do by joining bills. دانه پاني dāna pānī, s. m. Food, victuals, meat and drink. دانه دار dāna-dār, 1. Granulated. 2. Having the appearance of being granulated. 3. Containing grain. دانه دان کرنا dāna dān karnā, To mingle, confound or destroy. دانه کيش dāna-kesh, s. m. A kind of embroidered neckcloth worn in cold weather over the cloak.
subs: To cause a pigeon to bring up the grain in its crop (an act had recourse to to make a pigeon light for flying)
v.n. To caress by joining bills (as pigeons do), to bill
s.m. Billing, caressing (as pigeons do) by joining bills
s.f. A cursory or rough survey; a partial measurement or weighment (of the produce of a field); an assessment of the revenue upon a valuation of the standing crops
v.t. To feed (its young, a bird)
s.m. Meat and drink, provisions, food, victuals
(-meṅ), To put seed (into the ground), to sow; — to bait (a hook)
vulg. dāne-dār, adj. Granulated; having the appearance of being granulated; — containing grain; — apportioning jamʻ or any other contribution according to the actual produce
v.t. To mingle, confound, to destroy
(-ke āge or sāmhne), dāna denā (-ko), To throw grain (before), give grain (to), to feed (fowls, &c.)
s.m. One who clutches at, or who grudges, even a single grain; a miser, an avaricious person
s.m. A kind of embroidered neck-cloth (worn in cold weather over the cloak).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَانَه (dāna), from Proto-Indo-European *dʰoHnéh₂. Cognate with Sanskrit धान्य (dhānya).

s.m. 'A hero in liberality,' an exceedingly liberal man
adj. Worthy of a gift, meriting a donation.

def: adj. & s.m. Wise, learned; — a wise man, a sage (pl. dānāyān)
more: adj. Wise; sage. Nādān dost se dānā dushman bhalā . Prov. Better a clever foe than an ignorant friend. dānā bīnā , adj. Wise; learned. Ham keh sasur ke dānā bīnat haīn? What base father-in-law's gram have I stolen? (Said by an illiterate groom who mistakes P. dānā bīnā , as he had been called, for H. danā bīnnā gram pickings.) dānāyān-i-faraṅg . The wise men of Europe.
n. m. S. दानव, Pālī, dānā vo . A demon; Titan; giant.
adj. Wise, learned, sage. داناي دهر dānā-e-dahr, Wise. (Pers. plur. دانايان). ¶ For دانه dāna, Grain, &c. q. v.
subs: The learned of the age or time

def: s.m. corr. of dānav, q.v.

ety: for A. دناَءة, inf. n. of دنأ 'to be low, or ignoble,' &c.
def: s.f. Lowness, meanness, baseness.
(from دنؤ) s. f. Meanness, baseness.
s.f. Report, reverberation, booming (of cannon, &c.).
more: n. f. Boom of cannon, etc.

def: s. f. Wisdom.
s.f. Wisdom, knowledge.
more: n. f. Wisdom; sagacity; good sense.

def: s.f. The arabicized form of the next, q.v.
more: s. A weight, the sixth part of a dram or dirham: see دانک dāng.

def: part. adj. & s.m. Knowing, intelligent; skilled; — one (or he) who knows ( = jānne-wālā).
s.f. Giving, bountifulness, liberality ( = dānpan).
s.m. A class of demons; a demon; a giant, a Titan; — adj. Belonging, or peculiar, to the Dānavas.
incorr. for danāʼat, q.v.
s.m. Oppression, injustice; injury; — punishment, penalty; fine mulct; — an oar; (see ḍāṅḍ).
more: s. m. Fine, punishment, amercement, oppression, injustice, injury.

def: s.m. The beam of a weaver's loom.

s.m. Stick, staff, rod; mace; stem, handle; a pole or measure (of four cubits); a measure of time (twenty-four minutes); — assault, attack, violence; correction, corporal chastisement, punishment; amercement, fine, penalty; — danḍāśram (˚ḍa+āś˚), s.m. The order of the staff, condition of a pilgrim
more: s. m. 1. A stick, a staff. 2. Fine, penalty, punishment, castigation by amercement or putting to death. 3. (dakh.) See ڐنڐ ḍanḍ.
subs: s.m. 'Stick - assault,' actual violence, assault and battery
s.m. A small kind of fish, Cyprinus barbiger (?)

s.f. A copper or metallic cup of prescribed capacity and perforated by a small hole at the bottom (when placed in a vessel of water it answers the purpose of a clepsydra, and the water gradually filling it marks the time that has elapsed)
s.f. A kind of kettle-drum, on which the hours are struck
adj. & s.m. Awarding or inflicting punishment; — one who administers correction or punishment; an avenger
s.f. The rule of inflicting punishment, the system of civil and military administration taught by Ćāṇakya and others

danḍ-vat, s.f. lit. 'Like a stick'; falling or lying prostrate, prostration, a mode of salutation among Hindūs, obeisance, bow (the complete prostration is now rarely practised, except before an idol, or a person of high caste or position)
To fall or lie prostrate, to do obeisance
adj. Worthy or deserving of punishment, guilty.
wety: From Sanskrit दण्ड (daṇḍa).

def: s.m. A stick, staff, cudgel, pole; a measure of length, a pole of four to six cubits; a particular measure of time, about twenty-four minutes; — an oar; the back-bone; a line; — punishment, amercement, fine, forfeit; retaliation, revenge; — high land (not culturable for lack of water; hence), sterile land
more: s. m. 1. Retaliation, punishment, penalty, fine, amercement, mulct, revenge, forfeit. 2. An oar. 3. The backbone. 4. A stick. 5. A line. ڐانڐ بهرنا ḍānḍ bharnā, v. a. To pay a fine. ڐانڐ لينا ḍānḍ lenā, To take a fine, to fine, to amerce.
subs: To pay a fine, undergo a penalty or punishment, to suffer
To ply an oar, to row
(-se), To take a fine (from), to fine, amerce, punish; to take revenge, to retaliate

def: s.m. A stick, cudgel, rod; — the part of the arm above the elbow; — an athletic or gymnastic exercise (placing the hands on the ground and then bending down so as almost to touch the earth with the breast); — punishment, fine, amercement, mulct, compensation; retaliation; tax
more: s. m. 1. The arm above the elbow. 2. A kind of exercise, placing the hands on the ground and then bending down so as almost to touch the earth with the breast. ڐنڐ پيل ḍanḍ-pel, One who exercises himself at the ḍanḍ.
subs: (-kā), To pay a fine, to make compensation, pay damages (for)
s.m. One who exercises himself at the ḍanḍ
To practise the gymnastic exercise of ḍand
(-par), To levy or impose a fine, or tax
adv. With the power of the arm and head, with all one's might
adj. Deserving of punishment, punishable, penal.

def: s.m. A tooth; a rib; a toothed instrument (used by weavers); anything styptic or astringent (that contracts the mouth); name of a purgative seed (prob. croton; cf. dantī); name of a certain herb; — a class of beggars whose practice it is to cut and wound their bodies if their applications are not immediately attended to; — a thief; — adj. Poor, needy, in want of food; foolish, ignorant, stupid, silly; bold, fearless, wilful; unconscionable; irreligious.
more: n. m. The beam of a weaver's loom.

n. m. S. दण्ड 1. A rod; stick. 2. The arm above the elbow. Bhāiyoṅ ke ḍaṅḍ malo! Prov. Rub down the brethren's arms. (Support or keep in with your own). 3. An athletic exercise—placing the hands on the ground and then bending down so as almost to touch the earth with the breast. Sau ḍaṅḍ, na ek lipṭaṅt . Prov. One wrestler to a hundred ḍands! 4. Punishment; compensation for injury; mulct; damages; ransom; fine. 5. A tax. 6. Rus . The rent or revenue payable. ḍaṅḍ bharnā, yā denā , v. n. To pay damages; make good; pay a fine. ḍaṅḍ pelnā , v. a. To exercise oneself in ḍaṅḍ , q. v. ḍanḍ ḍālnā , v. a. To levy or impose a tax. ḍaṅḍ se chhūṭnā , v. n. To be let off the payment of a fine or tax. ḍaṅḍ lenā , v. n. To amerce; to levy a fine. ḍaṅḍ muṅḍ, yā ḍaṅḍe munḍe ke bal se , adv. By might of hand and head; with all one's might. ḍaṅḍ-yog , adj. Punishable; penal. ḍaṅḍī āgyā . Penal provision.

1. s. A rib, a toothed instrument used by weavers, a tooth, any astringent that contracts the mouth, name of a very purgative grain or seed, a sort of beggars that wound themselves if something be not soon given them, a sort of herb or grass. 2. adj. Poor, needy, foolish, ignorant, fearless, self-willed, thief, irreligious.

def: n. m. S. दण्ड a stick. 1. A stick; staff; cudgel. 2. A measure of length; a pole of four to six cubits. 3. An oar. 4. A measure of time; twenty-four minutes. 5. High land, not culturable for lack of water; upland fields, opp. of khādar. ḍāṅḍ lagānā yā mārnā , v. a. To ply an oar; row.
more: n. 1. A landmark; field boundary; boundary line. 2. Raised ground or ridge; a path for cattle. ḍāṅḍā meṅḍā , n. 1. A boundary road between two estates; bar (of a harbour). 2. Alliance; friendship. ḍāṅḍe meṅḍe kī takrār ; Ped. sarhaddī tanāzo , n. f. Boundary cases or disputes.

def: s.m. A landmark, a boundary line
subs: s.m. Frontier or boundary (between the lands of two proprietors, &c.).

def: s.m. Stick, club; pole (of a carriage), post, stake, &c. ( = ḍanḍā, q.v.)
subs: s.f. Fighting with sticks or staves, mutual cudgelling; single-stick.

def: s.m. Boundary line, field-boundary, landmark; raised ground, a ridge (of earth); a path, a road
subs: s.m. Frontier-boundary, or a boundary road, between two estates; — bar (of a harbour); — alliance, friendship.

def: s.m. A staff, club, bat, ensign-staff, pole, bar, beam (of a pair of scales); — the upper arm ( = ḍanḍ); — a wall enclosing a house; — a collector of market-dues ( = ḍanḍiyā)
more: s. m. 1. A staff, an ensignstaff. 2. The beam of a pair of scales. 3. A collector of market duties. See ڐنڐيا sign. 2.
subs: v.t. To lift one up by the arms and legs (representing a ḍolī with its pole)

s.f. A mode of torture (it consists in fastening a man's arms behind his back with a cord, which is twisted round by means of a stick so as to increase the tension until the pain inflicted becomes intolerable)
adj. (f. -ī), Like a stick; cylindrical; (in Bot.) terete
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀟𑀁𑀟𑀕 (ḍaṃḍaga), from Sanskrit दण्डक (daṇḍaka), from दण्ड (daṇḍa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-). Cognate with Hindi डाँड़ (ḍā̃ṛ) and दंड (daṇḍ), Bengali ডান্ডা (ḍanḍa), and Punjabi ਡੰਡਾ (ḍaṇḍā).

def: s.m. A rib; — the middle of a mountain; — adj. Lost.
more: n. m. 1. A staff; club; bat; an ensign's staff. 1. Sardārī kā ḍaṅḍā aṭkā hai . Prov. The badge of office still sticks to you. (Said to one who wont condescend to a lower place than he formerly held). 2. Sau ḍaṅḍī na ek Buṅdelkhaṅḍī . Prov. A hundred clubs to one Bundelkhandī! (He is so strong). 2. The wall round a house. ḍaṅḍā ḍolī k. , v. a. To lift one up by his arms and legs. ḍaṅḍā-sā , ( Bot .) Terete; cylindrical. ḍaṅḍā kheṅchnā , v. a. To build a wall. ḍaṅḍe bajāte phirnā , v. a. To wander about. ḍaṅḍe khelnā, yā bajānā , v. a. To play on sticks. pālkī kā ḍaṅḍā , n. The pole of a palanquin. ḍaṅḍā-zanī , n. f. A mode of torture. It consits in fastening a man's arms behind his back with a cord which is twisted round by means of a stick so as to increase the tension until the pain inflicted becomes intolerable. Elliot .

def: s.m. Stick, staff, pole, &c. (see danḍ); — a species of metre (the stanza of which exceeds twenty-seven, and may extend to two hundred syllables); — name of a district in the Dakkhan, between the Narbadā and Godāvarī rivers (which, in the time of Rāmćandra, was a forest, and celebrated as a place of pilgrimage); — the inhabitants of this district
subs: (˚ka+ar˚), and danḍak-van, s.m. The Danḍaka forest (in which for some time Rāma resided; — see above).

def: s.f. The country of Danḍaka (see above).
A stick put up as a land-mark, and H. مينڐ A bank raised for the like purpose) s. m. The frontier or boundary between the lands of two proprietors.
s.m. A tooth
more: s. m. A tooth. دندان گير dandān-gīr, adj. Biting (horse). دندان مصري dandān-miṣrī, s. f. A kind of sweetmeat.
subs: To make a crushing reply or rejoinder, to silence (a person)
s.f. A kind of sweetmeat, barley-sugar.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دَنْدَان (dandān). Doublet of دانت (dā̃t).

def: n. m. S. दन्त A tooth. dandāṅ-shikan javāb denā , v. a. To shut up or silence one. dandāṅ-misrī , n. f. Barley sugar.
s.m. Tooth (of a saw, &c.); anything resembling a tooth; a notch, a jag
more: s. m. The tooth of a saw, &c.
subs: (-meṅ), Notches or jags to form (in, a knife, &c.), to become notched or jagged.

def: n. m. The tooth of a saw, etc.; a jag. Dekho! chāqū meṅ dandāne na paṛ jāëṅ!
v.n. To sing for joy, be in high spirits; to enjoy oneself, to be contented and independent, to live at ease.
more: v. n. To sing for joy; to be in high spirits, cock-a-whoop, etc. 1. Yār to jahāṅ jāte haiṅ dandanāte jāte haiṅ . Where'er I go, a free and easy jolly front I show. 2. Ek din baṛā ḍhol dandanā-ke, Kahne lagā sone ko sunā-ke . Sohan Lāl. One day the big drum turned exultingly, And thus the gold addressed contemptuously.
v. n. To enjoy oneʼs self, to be contented and independent, to live at oneʼs ease.

def: adj. Punishable, to be chastised, deserving punishment, liable to a fine.
s.m. A blister; a pimple.
more: s. m. A blister, a pimple.

def: corr. of ḍanḍwat, q.v. s.f. See s.v. danḍ. s.m. Oppression, injustice; injury; — punishment, penalty; fine mulct; — an oar; (see ḍāṅḍ).
adj. Standing erect.
(dakh.) Efforts of arms and heads.

ety: Prk. दंडिओ; S. दण्डिकः (˚इन्+कः); and Prk. दंडिओ; S. दण्ड+इका
def: s.m. A rower: a waterman ( = ḍānḍī); — s.f. A balance.
s.m. (f. -inī), A staff-bearer; a religious mendicant who carries a staff in his hand; an epithet of Yama; a mace-bearer ( = danḍin).
more: s. m. 1. A mendicant who carries a staff in his hand: see ڐنڐي ḍaṇḍī. 2. (dakh. i. q. دسته) Handle (?).

def: s.f. (local) = ḍanḍī (the more com. form, q.v.). s.m. A rower: a waterman ( = ḍānḍī); — s.f. A balance.
s.m. An oarsman, a rower.
s.f. The beam of a pair of scales; — a carpet swung on a pole and carried on the shoulders of two men (used for the conveyance of residents in the hills, and called in English 'a dandy').
s.m. A religious mendicant carrying a staff; anyone carrying a staff; — a weighman.
more: s. f. 1. A handle. 2. The beam of a pair of scales. 3. A mendicant who carries a staff in his hand. پگ ڐنڐي pag-ḍanḍī, s. f. A footpath, by-path, track.
s. f. The tube of the corolla of the Nyctanthes arbortristis, which is used for dying.
s. An ear-ring affixed to the border of the ear ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: s.f. Beam (of a pair of scales); — pole or shaft (of a vehicle); a lever; a handle; stalk, stem, &c. ( = ḍanṭhā, ḍaṅṭhī, q.v.), the tube of the corolla of the Nyctanthes arbortristis (used for dyeing); — the penis, yard; — an ear-ring affixed to the border of the ear; — a foot-path, path; — a line, stripe, &c. (see ḍanḍīr)
subs: s.m. Holder of the beam (of scales), weighman
(-kī), To press down the weight arm of a balance by a twist of the finger, to give short weight.

def: n. f. S. दण्डी 1. The beam of a pair of scales; a shaft. 2. ( Mech .) A lever (A. bairam ). 3. Membrum virile; ( Slang ) yard. 4. Length ( lambān ). 5. (pl. ḍaṅḍiyāṅ ). The tube of the corolla of the nyctanthes which is used for a yellow dye. Ek paise kī ḍaṅḍiyāṅ de ḍo! 6. A handle. 7. A mendicant who carries a staff in his hand. 8. A stack ( ṭāl ). 9. ( Bot .) Peduncle; pedicle. ḍanḍī aur pahiyā . Wheel and axle, or perpetual lever. ḍanḍī-dār , n. m. A weighman. ḍanḍī mārnā , v. a. To press down the weight arm of the beam by a twist of the finger; to give short weight. Methī bechūṅ, soā bechūṅ, aur bechūṅ chaulāī, Bīch bajār meṅ ḍanḍī mārūṅ, tau kuṅjṛe kī jāī . Play. Methī I sell, and soā and chaulāī too I cry, In open mart I'll do you or no grocer's daughter I.
n. f. A rower; a waterman.
more: n. m. An oarsman; a boatman. ḍāṅḍ dhurrā, dhar dhurrā , n. m. Village boundary.

n. f. A carpet seat swung on a pole which is borne on the shoulders of two porters, called in English a dandy and used for the conveyance of residents in the hills. Bayā ghoslā uṛāë jātā hai . The weaver-bird is flying with its nest. ☞ Native wit compares the dandy with its occupant to the weaver-bird's hanging nest.

def: v. a. (dakh.) 1. To fine, amerce, punish. 2. (i. q. کاوش کرنا) To investigate (?).
adj. (f. -ī), Inflicting punishment, punishing, chastising; — s.m. Chastiser, punisher.
s.m. One who carries a stick or staff; a mace-bearer ( = ćob-dār), &c. (see danḍī).

ety: ḍanḍī, 'line'+pleon. suff. īr = Prk. इल्ली = S. इली
def: s.f. Line, stripe, trace, score, lineament.
more: s. f. A line, stripe, score, lineament.

def: n. f. A line; stripe; score.
adj. Striped, marked with lines.
part. Punished, chastised, amerced, sentenced, mulcted, fined.
s.m. (lit. 'striped') A kind of garment for women, a mantilla; — a collector of market duties.
more: s. m. 1. A kind of garment for women. 2. A collector of market duties.

def: n. m. A mantilla.
s. m. (dakh.) See ڐنڐيا ḍanḍiyā.
(dakh.) Efforts of arms and heads.
v.t. To punish, chastise, inflict a penalty on, to mulct, fine.
more: v. a. (dakh.) 1. To fine, amerce, punish. 2. (i. q. کاوش کرنا) To investigate (?).

ety: ḍāṅḍ˚ = Prk. दंड़(इ) or दंड़े(इ = S. दण्डय(ति), rt. दण्ड्
def: v.t. To punish, fine, amerce; to pay off, take revenge on, retaliate on (e.g. ulṭe ćor koṭwāle ḍāṅḍe).
more: v. a. To retaliate, to take revenge, to fine, to punish.

def: v.t. = ḍāṅḍnā, q.v.; — (fr. the trans. ḍāṅnā), v.n. To suffer punishment; to be fined, be taxed.
